This operation underscores the government's zero-tolerance policy against narcotics.

India Vs Pakistan: How the two nations stack up when it comes to nuclear arsenals
description: India and Pakistan possess nearly identical nuclear arsenals, yet their doctrines diverge significantly. India adheres to 'No First Use' and aims for a minimum deterrent, while Pakistan's 'Full Spectrum Deterrence' counters India's conventional strength. India is expanding its nuclear triad, focusing on survivability and reach, while Pakistan emphasises tactical nuclear weapons and flexible options, raising escalation risks.

Saurabh Kalia was captured and brutally killed, the full scale of the incursion became clear.

Pune hospital bomb scare: Shoe seller held from train for planting IED to extort money
description: A 35-year-old shoe seller from Solapur, living in Pune, was arrested at Nagpur railway station for allegedly planting a low-intensity bomb at a Pune hospital. Shivaji Rathod, unable to afford treatment for a sexually transmitted disease, attempted to extort money by creating panic. He was apprehended by the ATS on a train after a manhunt.

title: 'She cheated on me, gave our son to someone else': Hyderabad man dies by suicide, uploads video
description: A 28-year-old cab driver died by suicide in Hayathnagar, with police registering an abetment to suicide case against a woman named Saritha. The deceased's mother alleged his death was due to Saritha's betrayal after promising marriage. A suicide note and video recordings indicated he believed Saritha had abandoned their son and married another man.

title: NCP goes all-Pawar: Sunetra takes charge as sons get key party roles; seniors sidelined
description: NCP president Sunetra Pawar's letter to the ECI, omitting key organizational posts for Praful Patel and Sunil Tatkare, has sparked speculation. Sources indicate Sunetra Pawar and her sons are seeking a meeting with Tatkare to clarify the issue. Pawar previously attributed similar omissions to technical mistakes.

title: Ex-Google CEO Eric Schmidt has message for engineers who are still coding the old way
description: Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt declared the era of manual code writing is over, citing AI's explosive power. He believes programmers still using traditional methods are behind, as AI enables individuals to build powerful applications previously requiring large teams. This shift is causing a sell-off in software stocks, with companies needing to adapt quickly to survive.

title: After Microsoft gave engineers access to Anthropic's Claude Code, company is canceling licenses
description: Microsoft is phasing out internal licenses for Anthropic's Claude Code AI tool, shifting developers to its own GitHub Copilot CLI platform. This strategic move, effective by the end of June, aims to streamline AI coding tools and align with Microsoft's engineering needs and financial year-end.
